$ cat manifest.yaml
# Single source of truth for the SwiftDeploy stack.
# `swiftdeploy init` regenerates nginx.conf and docker-compose.yml from this file.
# Do not hand-edit the generated files; edit this file and rerun `init`.

services:
  image: swiftdeploy-stage4b-app:1.0.0
  port: 3000
  mode: stable
  version: "1.1.0"
  restart_policy: unless-stopped
  log_volume: swiftdeploy-logs

nginx:
  image: nginx:latest
  port: 18080
  proxy_timeout: 30
  error_contact: ops@swiftdeploy.local

network:
  name: swiftdeploy-net
  driver_type: bridge

opa:
  image: openpolicyagent/opa:1.16.1
  port: 18181

policy:
  infrastructure:
    min_disk_free_gb: 10
    max_cpu_load: 2.0
  canary:
    max_error_rate: 0.01
    max_p99_latency_seconds: 0.5
    window_seconds: 30

observability:
  history_file: history.jsonl
  audit_report: audit_report.md
  status_interval: 5

$ sha256sum nginx.conf docker-compose.yml
8ceedb2e5c67a8887172a8bc14db977d0c47a783db33e5219e806a238b65c0f3 *nginx.conf
d5cac7a072dbbf042a126d3d8245749ab8a80a64950c238c9400c0f3a3f82ab3 *docker-compose.yml

$ cat docker-compose.yml
# docker-compose.yml rendered by swiftdeploy from manifest.yaml.
# DO NOT hand-edit docker-compose.yml; edit manifest.yaml and rerun `swiftdeploy init`.

services:
  app:
    image: swiftdeploy-stage4b-app:1.0.0
    container_name: swiftdeploy-app
    environment:
      MODE: stable
      APP_VERSION: 1.1.0
      APP_PORT: "3000"
    networks:
      - swiftdeploy-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: "10001:10001"
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    volumes:
      - swiftdeploy-logs:/app/logs
    # Brief mandate: app service has no ports: mapping. Only nginx publishes.
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD
        - python
        - -c
        - "import os,sys,urllib.request; sys.exit(0 if urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:'+os.environ['APP_PORT']+'/healthz', timeout=2).status==200 else 1)"
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 6
      start_period: 5s

  nginx:
    image: nginx:latest
    container_name: swiftdeploy-nginx
    depends_on:
      app:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "18080:18080"
    networks:
      - swiftdeploy-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    cap_add:
      # nginx master needs CHOWN to own the worker pidfile and SETUID/SETGID
      # to drop the worker processes to the nginx user.
      - CHOWN
      - SETUID
      - SETGID
      - NET_BIND_SERVICE
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
    # Note: /var/log/nginx is intentionally NOT volume-mounted here; the
    # upstream nginx image symlinks access.log/error.log to stdout/stderr.
    # Mounting over it would break `docker compose logs nginx`.

  opa:
    image: openpolicyagent/opa:1.16.1
    container_name: swiftdeploy-opa
    command:
      - run
      - --server
      - --addr=0.0.0.0:8181
      - --log-level=info
      - /policies
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:18181:8181"
    networks:
      - swiftdeploy-net
    restart: unless-stopped
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    read_only: true
    volumes:
      - ./policies:/policies:ro

networks:
  swiftdeploy-net:
    name: swiftdeploy-net
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  swiftdeploy-logs:
    name: swiftdeploy-logs

$ cat nginx.conf
# nginx.conf rendered by swiftdeploy from manifest.yaml.
# DO NOT hand-edit nginx.conf; edit manifest.yaml and rerun `swiftdeploy init`.

upstream app_backend {
    server app:3000;
}

# Access log format mandated by the brief.
# ${request_time} escapes Python string.Template so the literal nginx variable
# $request_time survives rendering.
log_format swiftdeploy '$time_iso8601 | $status | ${request_time}s | $upstream_addr | $request';

server {
    listen 18080;
    server_name _;

    access_log /var/log/nginx/access.log swiftdeploy;
    error_log  /var/log/nginx/error.log warn;

    # Brief: identify swiftdeploy on every response.
    add_header X-Deployed-By swiftdeploy always;

    # Brief: forward upstream X-Mode header through to the client.
    # proxy_pass_header is required because nginx hides some upstream headers
    # by default; explicit pass-through is the defensible choice.
    proxy_pass_header X-Mode;

    proxy_connect_timeout 30s;
    proxy_send_timeout    30s;
    proxy_read_timeout    30s;

    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Host              $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP         $remote_addr;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For   $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://app_backend;
    }

    # Brief: JSON error bodies on 502/503/504. The "= @name" form replaces the
    # status code with our handler's status so we control the response shape.
    error_page 502 = @err502;
    error_page 503 = @err503;
    error_page 504 = @err504;

    location @err502 {
        default_type application/json;
        return 502 '{"error":"Bad Gateway","code":"502","service":"swiftdeploy-app","contact":"ops@swiftdeploy.local"}';
    }
    location @err503 {
        default_type application/json;
        return 503 '{"error":"Service Unavailable","code":"503","service":"swiftdeploy-app","contact":"ops@swiftdeploy.local"}';
    }
    location @err504 {
        default_type application/json;
        return 504 '{"error":"Gateway Timeout","code":"504","service":"swiftdeploy-app","contact":"ops@swiftdeploy.local"}';
    }
}
